name: AWS WAF - Amazon IP Reputation Managed Rule Group
objective: Attach the AWSManagedRulesAmazonIpReputationList managed rule group as a reusable standalone pack for blocking requests from AWS threat intelligence sources.
risk_mitigated: Known malicious scanners, credential stuffing infrastructure, botnet nodes, and other IPs that AWS threat intelligence identifies as high-risk sources.
potential_side_effects: Low expected false positive rate, but shared NAT, partner integrations, uptime monitors, and corporate egress gateways can occasionally appear in reputation feeds.
tuning_notes: Deploy with OverrideAction Count first. Review sampled requests and allow-list trusted business partner CIDRs with a higher-priority IPSetReferenceStatement before enforcing the managed rule group.
deployment_notes: Add this rule to an existing WebACL with a unique priority lower than broad application-layer managed groups. Keep visibility metrics enabled and switch OverrideAction from Count to None only after validation.
monitoring_notes: Track CountedRequests or BlockedRequests for k1nAwsIpReputationList, review sampled requests for critical business paths, and alert on sudden increases that may indicate credential stuffing or scanner traffic.

enforcement_transition.allowlist_priority_guidance: Place trusted partner or monitor IP set allow-list rules above this rule before switching to enforcement.
